tossups.question: In 2024, this composer’s 200th birthday was celebrated in an annual spring music festival that always begins on May 12th with his most famous orchestral work. A viola solo on a descending short-long-short motif over violin tremolos opens an E minor chamber piece by this composer that features a second movement polka. Another piece by this composer begins with two harps imitating the bard Lumír and uses the motif [read slowly] “B-flat, E-flat, D, B-flat” to depict a (*) “High Castle.” This non-Beethoven composer imitated his hearing loss with a sustained high E at the end of a string quartet that premiered with Dvořák as violist. Two flutes play swirling figures to represent a river in a set of six tone poems depicting the homeland of this composer of From My Life. For 10 points, name this Czech composer whose symphonic cycle Má vlast includes “The Moldau.”
tossups.question_sanitized: In 2024, this composer's 200th birthday was celebrated in an annual spring music festival that always begins on May 12th with his most famous orchestral work. A viola solo on a descending short-long-short motif over violin tremolos opens an E minor chamber piece by this composer that features a second movement polka. Another piece by this composer begins with two harps imitating the bard Lumir and uses the motif [read slowly] "B-flat, E-flat, D, B-flat" to depict a (*) "High Castle." This non-Beethoven composer imitated his hearing loss with a sustained high E at the end of a string quartet that premiered with Dvorak as violist. Two flutes play swirling figures to represent a river in a set of six tone poems depicting the homeland of this composer of From My Life. For 10 points, name this Czech composer whose symphonic cycle Ma vlast includes "The Moldau."
tossups.answer: Bedřich Smetana (The first line refers to the Prague Spring Festival. Moldau is the German name for the Vltava river.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Bedrich Smetana (The first line refers to the Prague Spring Festival. Moldau is the German name for the Vltava river.)

tossups.question: In a tone poem by this composer, the G-minor opening section is followed by a fugato (“foo-GAH-toh”) in A minor and 3/4 time that begins with the muted first violins playing a descending D6, G-sharp5 tritone. In a tone poem by this composer, the key signature switches for the first time from one sharp to four flats for an episode in which a flute duet plays over high, muted strings, titled “Moonlight. Dance of the Nymphs.” Another tone poem by him opens with two harps, one strumming, one playing cadenzas, depicting a mythical (*) bard. A tone poem by him adapts the Italian melody “La Mantovana” (“lah mahn-toh-VAH-nah”), which was later used for the Israeli national anthem, and depicts a river flowing by a castle called Vyšehrad (“vee-SHEH-rahd”). For 10 points, name this Czech composer whose cycle of six tone poems, Má Vlast (“mah vlahst”), includes “The Moldau” (“MOLE-dow”).
tossups.question_sanitized: In a tone poem by this composer, the G-minor opening section is followed by a fugato ("foo-GAH-toh") in A minor and 3/4 time that begins with the muted first violins playing a descending D6, G-sharp5 tritone. In a tone poem by this composer, the key signature switches for the first time from one sharp to four flats for an episode in which a flute duet plays over high, muted strings, titled "Moonlight. Dance of the Nymphs." Another tone poem by him opens with two harps, one strumming, one playing cadenzas, depicting a mythical (*) bard. A tone poem by him adapts the Italian melody "La Mantovana" ("lah mahn-toh-VAH-nah"), which was later used for the Israeli national anthem, and depicts a river flowing by a castle called Vysehrad ("vee-SHEH-rahd"). For 10 points, name this Czech composer whose cycle of six tone poems, Ma Vlast ("mah vlahst"), includes "The Moldau" ("MOLE-dow").

tossups.question: This city is the epithet of a Breton choirmaster whose motets were incompletely edited by George Nugent. A Jewish violinist from this city wrote 33 polyphonic settings of Hebrew liturgical music, punningly titled “Songs of Solomon.” L’Ottuso Accademico defends a compositional style first used in this city in the book On the Imperfections of Modern Music. Samuel Cohen set a poem by Naftali Herz Imber to the tune of a madrigal named for this city. While working in this city, a composer was criticized by Giovanni (*) Artusi for a style of song in which lyrics govern music. “Hatikvah” and Ma Vlást separately adapted the tune of a madrigal named for this birthplace of seconda pratica. Before moving to Venice from this city, one composer wrote three of his nine madrigal books and the oldest regularly performed opera. For 10 points, name this city where the early phase of Claudio Monteverdi’s career was patronized by the Gonzagas.
tossups.answer: Mantua [or Mantova; accept “La Mantovana” or “Il Ballo di Mantova” or “Mantuan Dance”; prompt on “Fuggi, Fuggi” by asking “what is that madrigal’s alternate title?”] (The first clue refers to Jacquet of Mantua. The second clue refers to Salomone Rossi. “Hatikvah” is not directly based on “Vltava;” Cohen used the Romanian adaptation of “La Mantovana,” “Carul cu boi,” while Smetana used its Czech adaptation, “Kočka leze dírou.”)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Mantua [or Mantova; accept "La Mantovana" or "Il Ballo di Mantova" or "Mantuan Dance"; prompt on "Fuggi, Fuggi" by asking "what is that madrigal's alternate title?"] (The first clue refers to Jacquet of Mantua. The second clue refers to Salomone Rossi. "Hatikvah" is not directly based on "Vltava;" Cohen used the Romanian adaptation of "La Mantovana," "Carul cu boi," while Smetana used its Czech adaptation, "Kocka leze dirou.")

tossups.question: In a letter explicating this piece to its composer’s friend, the number of performers is said to be purposefully set to discuss “what so obviously troubles me.” This piece’s second movement contains a “solo quasi Tromba” on the C string, followed immediately by a transposition to the (+) G string. The third movement of this piece opens with an exposed cello solo descending from an F above the staff to a sequence of trills below the staff. After an opening sforzando staccato chord followed by eighth note oscillations, this piece introduces a melody consisting of descending repetitions of the rhythm pickup eighth note, half note, low quarter note in the solo (*) viola. In the second section of this piece’s final movement, harsh, quiet tremolos underlie a long harmonic high E in the first violin representing its composer’s deafness. For 10 points, name this E minor chamber piece by the composer of Má vlast.
tossups.answer: Bedřich Smetana’s String Quartet No. 1 [or “From My Life” or „Z mého života” or “Aus meinem Leben” or JB 1:105 or T 106; accept Smetana’s String Quartet in E minor before the last line and prompt after]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Bedrich Smetana's String Quartet No. 1 [or "From My Life" or "Z meho zivota" or "Aus meinem Leben" or JB 1:105 or T 106; accept Smetana's String Quartet in E minor before the last line and prompt after]

tossups.question: In a string quartet by this composer, the opening viola solo starts with [read slowly] an eighth note B, a whole note B, then drops down to E, then plays an eighth note F-sharp, a whole note G, then down to B. The motif [read slowly] B-flat, E-flat, D, B-flat appears in an orchestral piece by this composer that opens with a harp solo, followed by a brass chorale. In a piece by this composer, the theme [read slowly] B, E, F-sharp, G, A, B, B appears after an opening in which two flutes play undulating E minor sixteenth note figures to represent waters; that piece appears in a cycle after one depicting the castle Vyšehrad (“VISH-eh-raht”). In a string quartet by this man, a violin plays a high sustained E representing his tinnitus and oncoming deafness. For 10 points, name this composer of the “From My Life” quartet and The Moldau, which is among the six tone poems of his Má Vlast.

tossups.question: This composer dedicated his piano piece “Faded Happiness,” the first of his six Reves, to a former student in the Thun family. In the second movement of this composer’s first string quartet, the viola is instructed to play a polka like a trumpet. He composed the concert etude On the Seashore, and wrote his Piano Concerto in G Minor to mourn the death of his daughter Bedriska. This composer wrote a string quartet in which a violin plays a long harmonic E after the rest of the quartet suddenly stops and plays a low tremolo in a section representing his loss of hearing. His symphonic poems include Sarka, as well as a piece which begins with two flutes representing streams that converge to the namesake river. For 10 points, name this Czech composer of the string quartet “From My Life,” who included his symphonic poems “Vysehrad” and “The Moldau” in Ma Vlast.
tossups.question_sanitized: This composer dedicated his piano piece "Faded Happiness," the first of his six Reves, to a former student in the Thun family. In the second movement of this composer's first string quartet, the viola is instructed to play a polka like a trumpet. He composed the concert etude On the Seashore, and wrote his Piano Concerto in G Minor to mourn the death of his daughter Bedriska. This composer wrote a string quartet in which a violin plays a long harmonic E after the rest of the quartet suddenly stops and plays a low tremolo in a section representing his loss of hearing. His symphonic poems include Sarka, as well as a piece which begins with two flutes representing streams that converge to the namesake river. For 10 points, name this Czech composer of the string quartet "From My Life," who included his symphonic poems "Vysehrad" and "The Moldau" in Ma Vlast.

bonuses.leadin: Over the course of 1874, this composer went from full hearing to total deafness. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Over the course of 1874, this composer went from full hearing to total deafness. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Czech nationalist composer of The Bartered Bride. During his decade of deafness, he wrote his tone poem cycle Má vlast, which contains the “The Moldau.”
Smetana depicted his deafness and subsequent tinnitus with a high E in the finale of this autobiographical chamber work.

The last word of the tempo indication for the second movement of the “From My Life” quartet advertises that the movement is in this genre. Smetana wrote a set of three “poetic” pieces in this genre for solo piano.

polka [or polky; accept “Allegro moderato à la polka”; accept Three Poetic Polkas; accept Polkas poétiques; accept Poetické polky]

polka [or polky; accept "Allegro moderato a la polka"; accept Three Poetic Polkas; accept Polkas poetiques; accept Poeticke polky]

bonuses.leadin: After hearing this opera during its 1916 run in Prague, a Czech author went on to translate most of the composer’s operas into German, finally granting them international success. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: After hearing this opera during its 1916 run in Prague, a Czech author went on to translate most of the composer's operas into German, finally granting them international success. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this opera. The Kostelnička (“KOSS-tell-NEECH-kuh”) murders the baby of this opera’s title character, who marries Laca (“LAH-tsuh”) after he slashes her face with a knife to make her too ugly for Števa to marry.

Jenůfa was the first of many Janáček (“yuh-NAH-check”) operas translated by this Czech author, Janáček’s main advocate. He later wrote Die Musik Israels, the first comprehensive study of the music of Israel, where he settled after fleeing Prague in 1939.
Brod praised this earlier Czech composer for mixing nationalism and cosmopolitanism. Brod translated this composer’s opera The Bartered Bride.
parts_sanitized: Name this opera. The Kostelnicka ("KOSS-tell-NEECH-kuh") murders the baby of this opera's title character, who marries Laca ("LAH-tsuh") after he slashes her face with a knife to make her too ugly for Steva to marry.

Jenufa was the first of many Janacek ("yuh-NAH-check") operas translated by this Czech author, Janacek's main advocate. He later wrote Die Musik Israels, the first comprehensive study of the music of Israel, where he settled after fleeing Prague in 1939.
Brod praised this earlier Czech composer for mixing nationalism and cosmopolitanism. Brod translated this composer's opera The Bartered Bride.
answers_sanitized: Jenufa ("yeh-NOO-fuh") [or Jeji pastorkyna; or Her Stepdaughter]

bonuses.leadin: This city’s annual spring classical music festival is held in its historic concert hall, the Rudolfinum. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city. This city’s old castle is depicted in a tone poem that opens with a lone harp, depicting Lumír, playing the motif “B-flat, E-flat, [pause] short D, B-flat.”
The Prague Spring Classical Music Festival always opens with a performance of Má Vlast, a cycle of six tone poems by this deaf composer of The Bartered Bride.

This composer’s tone poem Prague is often paired with his A Summer’s Tale. This composer wrote the Asrael symphony in grief after his wife Otilie and her father, who was his mentor, died within 14 months of each other.
answers_sanitized: Josef Suk (Suk's father-in-law was Antonin Dvorak.)

bonuses.leadin: A piece by this composer was inspired by hearing the scarlet tanager’s song on a visit to Spillville, Iowa. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Czech composer of the New World Symphony.

Dvořák was the violist for the private premiere of this composer’s first string quartet, which is subtitled “From My Life.” This composer also wrote a set of six tone poems depicting his homeland.
answers: Bedřich Smetana (The tone poems make up Má vlast.)

answers: basset-horn [do not accept or prompt on “horn” alone]
